title: chenopodium album f. lanceolatum
intro: Chenopodium album f. lanceolatum, also known as white goosefoot or lamb's quarters, is a subspecies of the Chenopodium album plant, a type of annual or biennial herb belonging to the family Amaranthaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Chenopodium album f. lanceolatum include treating digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in folk medicine for its nutritional value.
